name: Wonton Soup
recipeIngredient: salt or light soy sauce to taste
1 tablespoon cornstarch dissolved in stock/water (4 Tbsp)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the wontons: March-chop the ground meat a few times to loosen its formation. Place it in a bowl and add the seasonings; mix until smooth. Divide into about 36 small portions and wrap them in the wonton wrappers. Wet the edges of the wrappers all around, place a small teaspoon of filling in the center, bring all four sides together into a point to make a good seal.
2. Bring 5 to 6 cups water to a boil in a large pot; reduce heat to medium high, drop in the wontons, and stir gently. When the water boils again, add 1 1/2 cups cold water; when it comes to a boil again, add another 1 1/2 cups cold water. When it comes to a boil this time, let it boil for about 2 minutes. The wontons are cooked when they float to the top.
3. Repeatedly adding cold water serves two purposes: It gives the wontons enough time to cook and rinses them (filled wontons should not be rinsed after cooking as noodles are).
4. After boiling, cover and store the wontons 2 or 3 days in the refrigerator. Submerge them in cold water to separate before adding to soup.
5. Complete the soup: Bring stock to a simmer over medium heat and drop the wontons into it. Add torn spinach; when the stock comes to a boil, reduce heat to medium low and simmer for about 2 minutes. Season to taste with salt or light soy sauce. Serve from a tureen or in individual bowls. Let each person add a few more drops of sesame oil to their serving.
